name: Running circular to the top of Almeces (PR-MU 10)
name_original: Running circular a la cima de Almeces (PR-MU 10)
region_intro: Jogging routes around Sierra de Ricote traverse a diverse landscape characterized by arid ravines, towering cliffs, and abundant vegetation. The winding Segura River nourishes fertile plains, offering level running surfaces alongside more challenging inclines in the mountainous areas. Half of Ricote's territory is forest, providing a network of trails for outdoor pursuits, with limestone walls adding to the dramatic scenery. Best jogging routes around Sierra de Ricote The most popular jogging route is Running circular to the top of Almeces (PR-MU 10), a 10.8 miles (17.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 8 minutes to complete. This difficult route ascends to the Almeces summit, offering panoramic views of the region. Another top favourite among local runners is Pico Almeces loop from Ricote, a difficult 12.2 miles (19.6 km) path. This route provides a challenging run through varied terrain, including significant elevation gain. Local runners also love the View of the Sierra de la Pila – Almeces loop from Ojós, a 13.8 miles (22.2 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes and offering views towards Sierra de la Pila, often completed in about 3 hours 11 minutes. Jogging around Sierra de Ricote is defined by its varied terrain, including paths along the Segura River, forested areas, and ascents to viewpoints like Almeces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate trails to more difficult mountain runs. The routes in Sierra de Ricote are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Sierra de Ricote's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Sierra de Ricote offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 65 trails documented on komoot. These range from easy paths to more challenging mountain runs, ensuring options for all fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners or families in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Yes, Sierra de Ricote has several easy running routes. While many trails offer a moderate to difficult challenge, there are 3 routes specifically categorized as easy, and 47 as moderate, providing accessible options for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous jog. The region's diverse terrain includes flat paths along the Segura River, which are generally more suitable for an easier pace.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain runs in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Absolutely. Sierra de Ricote is known for its challenging mountain runs, with 15 routes categorized as difficult. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900657182/running-circular-to-the-top-of-almeces-pr-mu-10" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular to the top of Almeces (PR-MU 10)&lt;/a&gt;, which ascends to the 1,124-meter summit of Almeces, offer significant elevation gain and panoramic views, perfect for experienced runners seeking a strenuous workout.

Q: Are there any running trails with waterfalls in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Yes, you can enjoy a run that includes a beautiful waterfall. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31479431" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Caputa Waterfall – Poza de Fuente Caputa loop from Río Mula&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that takes you past the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2651129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Caputa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, a unique spot with natural pools that provides a refreshing sight.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while jogging in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Jogging in Sierra de Ricote offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You'll encounter arid ravines, towering limestone cliffs, and abundant forests, as half of Ricote's territory is wooded. The winding Segura River creates lush, fertile plains with orchards, providing a green contrast to the more rugged mountainous areas. This varied topography ensures a dynamic and scenic running experience.

Q: Are there running routes that offer panoramic views in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Many running routes in Sierra de Ricote lead to stunning panoramic viewpoints. The summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4827909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almeces&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers spectacular vistas of the entire region. Other notable viewpoints include Mirador de La Calera and Mirador del Gurugú, with routes like the "Ruta de los Miradores" specifically designed to showcase these breathtaking sights.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Sierra de Ricote?
A: The running trails in Sierra de Ricote are highly rated by the komoot community, boasting an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. Over 1000 runners have explored the region's varied terrain using komoot, often praising the diverse landscapes, challenging ascents, and the beauty of the Segura River paths.

Q: Are there circular running routes in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Sierra de Ricote are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point without retracing your steps. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900657182/running-circular-to-the-top-of-almeces-pr-mu-10" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular to the top of Almeces (PR-MU 10)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a complete loop through the mountainous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Sierra de Ricote is suitable for running year-round, but the best seasons are typically spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, especially in the arid areas, while winters are generally mild, though mountain tops like Almeces can occasionally see snow, adding a unique seasonal beauty to your run.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sights along the running paths in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Yes, as you jog through Sierra de Ricote, you can encounter rich historical and cultural elements. The region has a strong Moorish heritage, visible in ancient irrigation systems and traditional water wheels along the Segura River. Roman sites related to water use can also be found near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2651129" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Caputa Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, adding an intriguing historical dimension to your run.

Q: Is Sierra de Ricote suitable for running with dogs?
A: While the guide doesn't specify dog-friendly rules for every trail, many natural areas in Spain allow dogs on trails, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local regulations or specific trail signs before heading out with your canine companion to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: How can I access the running trails in Sierra de Ricote using public transport?
A: Accessing some running trails in Sierra de Ricote via public transport can be challenging, as many trailheads are located in more remote natural areas. While major towns in the Ricote Valley may have bus services, direct public transport to specific trail starting points is limited. Planning your route in advance and checking local bus schedules for towns like Ricote or Ojós is recommended.

Q: Where can I find parking near the running trailheads in Sierra de Ricote?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead in Sierra de Ricote. Many popular starting points, especially near villages like Ricote or Ojós, will have designated parking areas. For more remote trails, you might find informal parking spots. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot or local maps for parking information before your run.
tips: At an altitude of 1,124 meters above sea level, the views from the top of Almeces are spectacular. Despite having many antennas in the surroundings, you can search to get some beautiful panoramic views.
